topic: Mosel cool climate slopes and acidity

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Mosel vineyards combine a cool northerly climate with steep, favourably exposed slopes.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Cool conditions slow respiration and preserve grape acids, while slope exposure improves sunlight interception enough to support ripening.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Riesling can retain high acidity and freshness while reaching aromatic ripeness in suitable sites.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: cool climate preserves acidity and steep exposure helps achieve ripeness.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
